title: Daily Market Digest - June 17, 2026
subtitle: Wall Street Reacts to Fed Decision and Shifting Tech Landscape

items.headline: U.S. Markets Drift Ahead of Fed Rate Decision; Dow Hits New High as Tech Sector Experiences Volatility
items.summary: U.S. stocks saw mixed movements on Wednesday as investors awaited the Federal Reserve's latest monetary policy decision, the first under new Chair Kevin Warsh. The Dow Jones Industrial Average continued its positive momentum, building on a record close from Tuesday, while the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite experienced some choppiness, particularly influenced by fluctuating tech and AI-related stocks. Optimism stemming from an interim U.S.-Iran deal, easing global oil supply concerns, provided a backdrop for cautious trading.

items.statContext: Up 0.1% early Wednesday; closed down 0.6% on Tuesday.
Up 0.2% early Wednesday; closed down 1.2% on Tuesday.
Up 0.4% early Wednesday; closed up 0.64% on Tuesday.

items.headline: La-Z-Boy Rises on Strong Earnings
items.summary: Furniture retailer La-Z-Boy jumped after reporting stronger-than-expected profit and revenue for its latest quarter.

items.headline: Applied Materials Surges on AI Demand
items.summary: Applied Materials climbed, bolstered by strong demand for AI infrastructure, following a broader upward trend in certain AI-related stocks.

items.headline: Broadcom Gains Amid AI Infrastructure Demand
items.summary: Broadcom saw significant gains as investor sentiment for AI infrastructure remained strong.

items.headline: BMW Drops on Cut Profit Outlook
items.summary: BMW shares fell to a five-year low after the German automaker reduced its 2026 profit outlook, citing weakening demand in China and disruptions from the Iran conflict.

items.headline: Carvana Sinks in Early Trading
items.summary: Online used car retailer Carvana experienced a notable decline in early trading.

items.headline: SpaceX Erases Early Gains, Falls After IPO Rally
items.summary: After an initial surge following its IPO and news of an AI startup acquisition, SpaceX shares erased early gains and dipped.

items.headline: Federal Reserve Holds Rates Steady at 3.5%-3.75%; Chair Warsh's First FOMC Meeting Concludes
items.summary: The Federal Reserve's Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) concluded its two-day meeting, unanimously deciding to maintain the target range for the federal funds rate at 3.5% to 3.75%. This marks the first FOMC decision under new Chair Kevin Warsh, with investors keenly analyzing his press conference for clues on future monetary policy and communication style. The committee noted economic activity is expanding at a solid pace despite elevated uncertainty from the Middle East, with inflation remaining high due to supply shocks, including energy.

items.headline: Strong Retail Sales Data Underscores Consumer Resilience
items.summary: U.S. retail sales recorded a robust 0.9% month-on-month increase in May, surpassing economist expectations and marking the fourth consecutive month of growth. This data suggests a resilient consumer base supporting the economy despite elevated inflation.

items.headline: Oil Prices Stabilize as U.S.-Iran Deal Eases Supply Concerns
items.summary: Oil prices experienced fluctuations but generally stabilized after sharp declines, driven by optimism over an interim agreement between the U.S. and Iran to end their conflict and potentially reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Brent crude, for instance, saw an early Wednesday rise of 0.7% to $79.53, following its dip below $80 a barrel on Tuesday amidst easing supply fears.

items.headline: CarMax Reports Q1 Earnings; Jabil Exceeds Expectations
items.summary: CarMax ($KMX) reported its first-quarter fiscal 2027 results, with net revenues increasing by 6.2% to $8.0 billion. Diluted earnings per share came in at $1.31, slightly below $1.38 a year ago. Meanwhile, Jabil ($JBL) exceeded analyst expectations in its latest quarter, partly due to strong AI infrastructure demand.
